moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Reduce QQQ and QQQI to 15% each, as both were flagged for trimming by the scanner.
Buy MSFT at 5% to add a high-quality tech name, diversifying slightly.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by cutting high-risk assets and reducing concentration.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall market exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and reduced volatility. It significantly lowers your risk profile by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower potential returns but also smaller drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove high-risk leveraged ETFs from the portfolio.
Reduce QQQ and QQQI to 15% each, as flagged for trimming by the scanner.
Buy NVDU and MSFT at 5% each, adding high-scoring tech setups to the book.
This plan balances risk by reducing leverage and concentration while adding quality growth names.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer, keeping total adds within limits.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces your overall leverage and concentration while introducing strong scanner setups. Expect moderate returns with manageable drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to the concentration limit, freeing up capital.
Sell SOXL and SPAL completely, as both are below their stops and flagged.
Reduce QQQ and QQQI to 10% each, as they were flagged for trimming.
Buy NVDU and MSFT at 10% each, leaning into the highest-scoring long setups.
Watch SQQQ for a potential 5% buy, as a tactical hedge if tech weakness emerges.
This plan aggressively reallocates capital to strong setups, maintaining strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It involves higher concentration in high-scoring setups and potentially using inverse leverage for hedging. Be aware that this mode significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown, and leveraged ETFs can experience daily decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, prioritizing stability.
Sell QQQ, SMH, and CRWD to reduce exposure to growth and high-beta assets.
Increase QQQI to 30% to boost monthly distributions and income focus.
This plan rotates the portfolio heavily into income-generating ETFs for steady distributions.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth, maintaining a cash buffer.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, aiming to park cash for yield. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by rotating into income and covered-call ETFs, reducing growth exposure. While prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown, remember that distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market selloffs.
